purpose: The parties intend to explore a potential collaboration involving the integration of Veridian's autonomous navigation modules into Stonebridge's warehouse logistics systems. Confidential information will be exchanged to evaluate technical feasibility, commercial terms, and operational requirements.
confidential_info_definition: Confidential Information means all non-public information disclosed by either party, whether in writing, orally, visually, or in electronic form. This includes but is not limited to: technical specifications, algorithms, source code, hardware designs, product roadmaps, manufacturing processes, customer lists, pricing strategies, financial projections, and business plans.
exclusions: Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available without breach of this agreement; (b) was known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure, as evidenced by written records; (c) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of the Confidential Information; (d) is lawfully received from a third party without restriction on disclosure.
obligations: The Receiving Party shall: (a) use Confidential Information solely for the stated purpose; (b) restrict access to employees and advisors with a need to know, each bound by confidentiality obligations no less protective than this agreement; (c) exercise at least the same degree of care used to protect its own confidential information, but no less than reasonable care; (d) not reverse-engineer, decompile, or disassemble any disclosed materials.
permitted_disclosures: Disclosure is permitted if required by law, regulation, or court order, provided the Receiving Party gives prompt written notice to the Disclosing Party and cooperates to seek protective measures.
confidentiality_duration: Obligations survive for 5 years following the end of the disclosure period.
return_clause: Within 30 days of written request or expiration of the disclosure period, the Receiving Party shall return or certify destruction of all Confidential Information, including copies, notes, and derivative materials. Retained copies in automated backup systems are exempt, provided they remain subject to confidentiality obligations.
remedies: The parties acknowledge that unauthorized disclosure may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages are insufficient. The Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other available legal or equitable remedies, without the requirement of posting a bond.
